issues.description: The BANNED-before-role invariant is only applied to the pre-upsert read. In the create path, `upsert` may take the update/collision branch and returns only `{ role }`; if the existing row is or becomes `restriction: "BANNED"` between the initial `findUnique` and the `upsert`, the page assigns `role = upserted.role` and renders `SpaceClient` without rechecking restriction. This leaves a race-shaped variant of the r1 BANNED-admin role leak, and the page tests currently cannot catch it because they mock upsert as returning role only.
issues.recommendation: Change the upsert select to include `restriction`, then re-apply the BANNED guard after upsert before passing role to `SpaceClient` (or make the create/read decision transactional with a postcondition check). Add a page test where initial membership lookup is null but upsert returns an OWNER/STAFF row with `restriction: "BANNED"` and assert redirect.
